LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LUCY NEEDHAM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE 3RD AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE THAT: (1) THERE WAS A CONTRACT BETWEEN THE PARTIES; AND (2) PLAINTIFF WAS A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED WHETHER THE CONTRACT WAS WRITTEN, ORAL OR IMPLIED BY CONDUCT. FOR THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 17200, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT LUCY NEEDHAM ENGAGED IN ANY UNFAIR OR UNLAWFUL BUSINESS PRACTICES; THE ALLEGATIONS ARE CONCLUSORY. FOR THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE EACH OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED AS MOOT SINCE DEFENDANT WITHDRAWS ITS REQUEST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO KENNETH MARSHALL'S MOTION TO REQUIRE CROSS COMPLAINANT TO FURNISH BOND
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF OCT-01-2014 CONTINUED TO NOV-12-2014 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT(S) TO BRING CROSS COMPLAINT(S) AT ISSUE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT KEN MARSHALL'S AMENDED MOTION TO REQUIRE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TO FURNISH BOND IS DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT KEN MARSHALL HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT "THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THE PROSECUTION OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT AGAINST THE MOVING PARTY WILL BENEFIT THE CORPORATION OR ITS SHAREHOLDERS." (CAL. CORP. CODE SEC. 800(C).) THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE DECLARATION OF KENNETH MARSHALL FILED IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION BECAUSE IT WAS NOT EXECUTED IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SEC. 2015.5, REQUIRING THAT A DECLARATION MUST BE MADE UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY UNDER THE LAWS OF CALIFORNIA. (SEE ALSO KULSHRESTHA V. FIRST UNION COMMERCIAL CORP. (2004) 33 CAL.4TH 601, 617-18.). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LUCY NEEDHAM'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY FEES (FAC, P. 17:20), AS TO THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS (FAC, P. 2:18-19, 3:11-16) AND DEFENDANT'S PERSONAL LIFE (FAC, P. 8:22 - 9:16, EXHIBIT F). PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED THE STATUTORY OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR ATTORNEY FEES. (CCP 1021.) THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS ARE CONCLUSORY. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION THAT IT WILL STIPULATE TO REMOVE REFERENCES TO DEFENDANT'S PERSONAL LIFE, AND THAT IT WILL CONFER WITH DEFENDANT ON THIS SUBJECT. LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS FOODCALC LLC, GEOFFREY NEEDHAM, MAUREEN NEEDHAM AND LUCY NEEDHAM'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP 430.10(E).) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE BREACH OF AN IMPLIED CONTRACT. "IF FACTS APPEARING IN THE EXHIBITS CONTRADICT THOSE ALLEGED, THE FACTS IN THE EXHIBITS TAKE PRECEDENCE." (HOLLAND V. MORSE DIESEL INTERN., INC. (2001) 86 CAL.APP.4TH 1443, 1447.) THE FACTS IN THE EXHIBITS DEMONSTRATE THAT THERE WAS NO MUTUAL ASSENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. (SEE SAC, EXHIBITS A - C.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion To Require X-Complainants To Furnish Bond
Off Calendar Sep-19-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

CROSS DEFENDANT KEN MARSHALL's Motion To Require Cross-Complainants To Furnish Bond is Off calendar. Duplicative entry. =(302/EHG)

Amended Notice Of Motion To Require X-Complainants To Furnish Bond
Off Calendar Sep-19-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Cross-Defendant Ken Marshall's Amended Motion To Require Cross-Complainants To Furnish Bond is Denied. Cross-Defendant Ken Marshall has not met his burden of establishing that "there is no reasonable possibility that the prosecution of the cause of action alleged in the complaint against the moving party will benefit the corporation or its shareholders." (Cal. Corp. Code sec. 800(c).) The Court did not consider the Declaration of Kenneth Marshall filed in support of this motion because it was not executed in compliance with CCP sec. 2015.5, requiring that a declaration must be made under penalty of perjury under the laws of California. (See also Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 617-18.). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAFAEL R. PACQUING, TEL. NO. (626) 975-1488 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRETT LOCKER OF THE SCHLEY LOOK & LOCKER LLP, TEL. NO. (805) 963-4929 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FOOD CAL, LLC, BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS FOODCALC LLC, GEOFFREY NEEDHAM, MAUREEN NEEDHAM AND LUCY NEEDHAM'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained, without leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).) Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege breach of an implied contract. "If facts appearing in the exhibits contradict those alleged, the facts in the exhibits take precedence." (Holland v. Morse Diesel Intern., Inc. (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1443, 1447.) The facts in the exhibits demonstrate that there was no mutual assent between the parties. (See SAC, Exhibits A - C.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

ATTY. BRETT LOCKER OF THE SCHLEY LOOK & LOCKER LLP, TEL. NO. (805) 963-4929 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FOOD CAL, LLC, BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS FOODCALC LLC, GEOFFREY NEEDHAM AND MAUREEN NEEDHAM'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted, without leave to amend. Second Amended Complaint, 4 - 5 are stricken pursuant to the stipulation of the parties. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2013 Continued To Mar-05-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RAFAEL PACQUING, ESQ., 626.975.1488, FOR PLAINTIFF FOODCARE INC.
BRETT LOCKER, 805.963.4929, FOR DEFENDANT LUCY NEEDHAM.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT LUCY NEEDHAM'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Granted, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the prayer for attorney fees (FAC, p. 17:20), as to the alter ego allegations (FAC, p. 2:18-19, 3:11-16) and Defendant's personal life (FAC, p. 8:22 - 9:16, Exhibit F). Plaintiff has not pled the statutory or contractual basis for attorney fees. (CCP 1021.) The alter ego allegations are conclusory. Plaintiff concedes in opposition to the motion that it will stipulate to remove references to Defendant's personal life, and that it will confer with Defendant on this subject. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RAFAEL PACQUING, ESQ., 626.975.1488, FOR PLAINTIFF FOODCARE INC.
BRETT LOCKER, 805.963.4929, FOR DEFENDANT LUCY NEEDHAM.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION: DEFENDANT LUCY NEEDHAM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the 3rd and 7th causes of action, and overruled as to the 1st, 2nd, 4th and 5th causes of action. For the 3rd cause of action for breach of contract, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege that: (1) there was a contract between the parties; and (2) Plaintiff was a third party beneficiary of the contract. Plaintiff has not pled whether the contract was written, oral or implied by conduct. For the 7th cause of action for violation of Business & Professions Code 17200, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege that Defendant Lucy Needham engaged in any unfair or unlawful business practices; the allegations are conclusory. For the 1st, 2nd, 4th and 5th causes of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each of these causes of action. The request for judicial notice is denied as moot since Defendant withdraws its request. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
